fresh_facts.claim: Iran shot down a U.S. fighter jet on April 3-4, 2026, with one crew member rescued and search ongoing for the second crew member.
U.S. airstrikes destroyed Iran's largest bridge on April 3-4, 2026, killing eight people.
U.S.-Israeli airstrikes hit a major Iranian public health facility on April 3-4, 2026.
Iranian drones struck Kuwaiti oil refinery and desalination plant on April 3-4, 2026.
Thousands of additional U.S. troops are deploying to the Middle East as of April 3-4, 2026.
Three U.S. generals, including former Army Chief of Staff Randy A. George, were removed from their positions without explanation on April 3, 2026.
U.S. intelligence assessed that Iran maintains about half its missile launchers intact as of April 3-4, 2026.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei, son of Ali Khamenei, was appointed as Supreme Leader by Iran's Assembly of Experts following his father's death.
Iran appointed hardliner Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, former IRGC deputy, as Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing Ali Larijani who was killed.
President Trump announced productive phone talks with a top Iranian official led by envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner on April 4, 2026.
The U.S. postponed attacks on Iranian energy sites for five days as part of diplomatic negotiations as of April 4, 2026.
Pakistan delivered a U.S. 15-point peace plan to Iran covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran is reviewing but not currently negotiating the U.S. peace plan as of April 4, 2026.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faced backlash from hardliners for signaling conditional willingness to end the war as of April 4, 2026.
Iranian hardliners branded former Foreign Minister Mohammad Javad Zarif a traitor and spy, with MPs urging his arrest.
Iranian Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied on social media that Iran is holding U.S. talks as of April 4, 2026.
Iran experienced severe medicine shortages with insulin prices reaching 70 million rials (approximately $46.7) as of April 4, 2026.
War-driven oil price surges are exacerbating economic crises across Asia, including in the Philippines as of April 4, 2026.
Iran's judiciary executed Amirhossein Hatami on Thursday for a Basij base fire from January unrest, signaling regime prioritization of survival.
Amnesty International accused Iran of recruiting children as young as 12 years old as combatants, constituting a war crime.
Israel accelerated strikes against Iran, targeting Tehran electricity infrastructure ahead of potential war end as of April 4, 2026.
The U.S. is deploying 1,500 to 3,000 paratroopers to the Middle East by the Friday deadline as of April 4, 2026.
Egypt, Pakistan, and Turkey mediated diplomatic messages between the U.S. and Iran as of April 4, 2026.
The UN Security Council debated sanctions regarding Hormuz reopening, with China, France, and Russia objecting to a resolution as of April 4, 2026.
Trump threatened further strikes on Iranian bridges and power plants as of April 3-4, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Escalating military strikes continue with U.S. F-15 downed and Iranian drone attacks on Gulf infrastructure, while simultaneous diplomatic engagement through regional mediators proceeds under a five-day bombing pause; Mojtaba Khamenei's new Supreme Leadership faces severe internal fractures with hardliner appointments, civilian leadership backlash, medicine shortages intensifying, and Iran maintaining approximately half its missile capability despite heavy losses.
regime_fall.last_major_event: U.S. F-15 fighter jet shot down on April 3-4 with one crew member rescued while search continues for second, concurrent with U.S. destruction of Iran's largest bridge and strikes on public health facility, Iranian drone attacks on Kuwaiti oil infrastructure, and Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed as new Supreme Leader by Assembly of Experts.
